headlines.title: China takes US crown on ranking of world’s fastest supercomputers
headlines.snippet: China's supercomputer, LineShine, has surpassed the US-based El Capitan to become the world's most powerful. This ranking is according to the latest TOP500 list.

headlines.title: Trump claims US recouped Venezuela war costs 28 times over
headlines.snippet: The United States has recovered its investment in oil extraction from Venezuela 28 times over, according to a claim made by US President Donald Trump.

headlines.title: Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison
headlines.snippet: A report by the Palestinian Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs Commission details that Palestinian women in Israel's Damon Prison are facing severe conditions. They are reportedly experiencing overcrowding, deliberate medical neglect, and denial of basic rights, including necessary care for pregnant prisoners.

headlines.title: Ben-Gvir says Israel may ‘act alone’ against Iran in split with US
headlines.snippet: Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir stated that the US is naive to expect Iran to abandon its nuclear program. He suggested that Israel might need to take independent action against this perceived threat.

headlines.title: UN report: Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
headlines.snippet: A UN commission of inquiry has concluded that Israel has committed genocide in Gaza, specifically by deliberately targeting children. The report indicates that Palestinian children have been intentionally killed by Israeli security forces.

headlines.title: Oman announces temporary maritime corridor to bypass Hormuz blockade
headlines.snippet: Oman has announced the establishment of a temporary maritime corridor to facilitate passage for vessels seeking to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative was coordinated with the International Maritime Organisation.

headlines.title: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions
headlines.snippet: An assessment by the UN's Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs indicates that approximately 80 percent of Gaza's population, nearly 1.7 million displaced Palestinians, are experiencing severe shortages of water, shelter, and essential services.
